_meta.description: Structured evidence database for Chrome extension and protocol service. Extends matrix.json with form detection, timing rules, warnings, and clinical dose targets.

citrate.side_effects: Osmotic laxative effect at higher doses

oxide.warning: Only ~4% absorbed. Most passes through unabsorbed. Not recommended for correcting deficiency.

l-threonate.warning: Very low elemental magnesium per dose. Not suitable for general deficiency correction.

taurate.warning: Marketed for heart health but NO human RCTs comparing to other forms. Theoretical rationale only (PMID 8692051).

deficiency.note: Dose depends on form. Bisglycinate effective at lower doses.

ferrous-sulfate.warning: Causes significant GI side effects. Iron bisglycinate has 2-3x better absorption with half the side effects.

bisglycinate.side_effects: 37% vs 21% side effect rate (much lower than sulfate)

ferrous-gluconate.side_effects: Better tolerated than sulfate but less studied than bisglycinate

carbonyl-iron.side_effects: Lower peak absorption, lower toxicity risk

cyanocobalamin.side_effects: Contains trace cyanide (harmless at supplement doses)
cyanocobalamin.warning: Synthetic form requiring metabolic conversion. Methylcobalamin is preferred for neuropathy (PMID 32716261, 15 RCTs).

general.note: Max 500mg per dose (absorption ceiling). Take divided doses.

best_for: budget option WITH food AND normal stomach acid
carbonate.warning: Absorbs 10x LESS than citrate in people with low stomach acid (Recker 1985 NEJM, PMID 4000241). Useless for PPI users, bariatric patients, and elderly with reduced acid.

ubiquinol.absorption: high (72% higher than ubiquinone in one crossover, PMID 27128225)

ubiquinone.note: Conversion to ubiquinol becomes less efficient with age. Both forms interconvert in the body.

ethyl-ester.warning: 70% less bioavailable than triglyceride form. Most cheap fish oils use this form. Check label carefully — if it doesn't say 'triglyceride form,' it's likely ethyl ester.

deficiency_correction.note: Dose by blood level. Target 40-60 ng/mL.

d3.absorption: high (2-3x more effective than D2 at raising blood levels)

d2.warning: 2-3x less effective than D3 at raising blood levels. D3 is preferred unless vegan.

best_for: everything — most researched supplement in existence

hcl.warning: 5-10x more expensive than monohydrate. Better solubility does NOT equal better absorption. No study has shown HCl superior to monohydrate. ISSN confirms monohydrate is the gold standard.

general.note: Dose is strain-dependent. CFU count alone is meaningless without strain specificity.

antibiotic_associated.note: L. rhamnosus GG or S. boulardii specifically studied

ibs.note: VSL#3 or B. infantis 35624 specifically studied

vaginal_health.note: Specific strains required: L. rhamnosus GR-1 + L. reuteri RC-14

immune.note: Best evidence for L. rhamnosus GG

travelers_diarrhea.timing: daily, start 5 days before travel through trip duration

best_for: condition-specific use with matching clinical evidence
strain_specific.side_effects: Generally mild GI adjustment in first 1-2 weeks

generic_blend.absorption: unknown — strain survivability untested for most blends
generic_blend.warning: High CFU counts are marketing, not science. AGA recommends against probiotics for most digestive conditions. Evidence is strain-specific — a '50 billion CFU blend' with unstudied strains has no clinical backing.

ksm-66.side_effects: Generally well-tolerated. Cycle 8 weeks on / 2 weeks off.

sensoril.side_effects: More sedating than KSM-66 — better for evening/sleep use

generic_powder.warning: Generic root powder has no standardized withanolide content. Clinical trials use KSM-66 or Sensoril extracts, not raw powder. You don't know what dose of active compounds you're getting.

general.note: Plus potassium 200-400mg and magnesium 50-100mg. Needs scale with sweat rate.

athletic.unit: mg sodium per hour of exercise
athletic.note: ACSM recommends replacing sweat losses. Heavy sweaters may need 1000-1500mg/hr.

fasting_keto.note: Ketosis increases sodium excretion. Must be sugar-free.

powder_with_sugar.note: Glucose-sodium co-transport enhances fluid absorption in the small intestine. This is the mechanism behind WHO ORS. Athletes and those rehydrating from illness benefit from small amounts of sugar.

hair_maintenance.note: Evidence is WEAK for non-deficient people (PMID 39148962)

d-biotin.warning: A 2024 review (PMID 39148962) found biotin for hair loss is 'not supported by high-quality studies' in non-deficient people. At $0.06/day it's cheap insurance, but don't expect miracles. Megadosing (>5000mcg) has no additional evidence. Can interfere with thyroid and troponin blood tests — stop 48-72 hours before bloodwork.

anxiety.note: Can be taken as needed for acute anxiety

key_studies.title: Hidese 2019 — L-theanine RCT for stress in healthy adults (n=30)
key_studies.finding: 200mg/day for 4 weeks reduced stress-related symptoms (PSQI sleep subscores improved, p<0.05)

key_studies.title: Everett 2020 — Systematic review of L-theanine for stress and anxiety (9 studies)
key_studies.finding: 200-400mg/day may assist in reducing stress and anxiety in stressful conditions

key_studies.title: Lyon 2011 — L-theanine RCT for sleep in ADHD boys (n=98)
key_studies.finding: 400mg/day significantly improved sleep efficiency and reduced activity during sleep vs placebo

key_studies.title: Sarris 2019 — L-theanine adjunctive for GAD RCT (n=46)
key_studies.finding: 450mg/day did not significantly outperform placebo for GAD (negative result)

key_studies.title: AlphaWave 2024 — L-theanine RCT for stress in healthy adults (n=100)
key_studies.finding: 200mg/day for 28 days reduced cortisol and improved stress measures vs placebo

key_studies.title: Cheah 2021 — Ashwagandha sleep meta-analysis (5 RCTs, n=400)
key_studies.finding: Small but significant sleep improvement, more prominent with insomnia diagnosis and doses >=600mg/day

testosterone.note: Standardized to 2-10% eurycomanone. Effect primarily in men with low-normal T.

standardized_extract.side_effects: Generally well-tolerated. Insomnia at high doses. Cycle 8 weeks on / 2 weeks off.

raw_powder.warning: Raw root powder has no standardized active compound content. Clinical trials use standardized extracts.

key_studies.title: Leisegang 2022 — Systematic review & meta-analysis of tongkat ali and testosterone (9 studies, 5 RCTs pooled)
key_studies.finding: Significant increase in total testosterone (SMD=1.352, 95% CI 0.565-2.138, p=0.001). Effect confirmed in hypogonadism subgroup.

key_studies.title: Talbott 2013 — RCT in moderately stressed adults (n=63)
key_studies.finding: 200mg/day for 4 weeks: cortisol -16%, testosterone +37%, tension -11%, anger -12% vs placebo.

key_studies.title: Tambi 2012 — Open-label in late-onset hypogonadism (n=76)
key_studies.finding: 200mg/day for 1 month: 90.8% of patients showed normal testosterone values after treatment.

key_studies.title: Firdaus 2021 — 6-month double-blind placebo-controlled RCT in ADAM (n=45)
key_studies.finding: 200mg/day + training significantly improved erectile function and increased total testosterone. No serious adverse events.

key_studies.title: George & Henkel 2014 — Review of phytoandrogenic properties
key_studies.finding: Tongkat ali may serve as alternative to TRT for testosterone deficiency syndrome via SHBG reduction and Leydig cell support.

key_studies.title: Yakubu 2005 — Fadogia agrestis aphrodisiac study in RATS (NOT human)
key_studies.finding: Rat study only. Testosterone increased dose-dependently over 5 days. Zero human data exists for fadogia.

key_studies.title: Yakubu 2008 — Fadogia agrestis testicular toxicity in RATS
key_studies.finding: 28-day rat study found adverse effects on testicular function at moderate and high doses.

key_studies.title: Yakubu 2009 — Fadogia agrestis liver/kidney toxicity in RATS
key_studies.finding: Liver and kidney enzyme changes at all doses over 28 days. Increased oxidative stress markers.

timing_rules.instruction: Take iron and calcium at LEAST 2 hours apart. Calcium inhibits iron absorption.
Take iron WITH vitamin C to enhance absorption (up to 6x improvement).
Take magnesium glycinate 30-60 minutes before bed for sleep benefit. Take with food to reduce GI effects.
Take fat-soluble supplements with a meal containing fat for best absorption.
Max 500mg calcium per dose. Absorption drops significantly above this. Split into 2 doses if taking 1000mg+.
For PPI users, metformin users, elderly, and bariatric patients: use sublingual B12 to bypass gut absorption issues.
If taking zinc >30mg/day long-term, add 1-2mg copper to prevent copper depletion.
Take multivitamin with your largest meal. Reduces nausea and improves absorption of fat-soluble vitamins.
Stop biotin 48-72 hours before any blood tests. High-dose biotin interferes with thyroid (TSH, T3, T4) and cardiac troponin assays, potentially giving false results.

glp1_user.name: GLP-1 / Ozempic / Wegovy / Mounjaro
glp1_user.rationale: Up to 40% of weight lost is muscle. 22.4% develop nutritional deficiency within 12 months (PMID 40584822). Joint advisory recommends 1.2-1.6g/kg protein + proactive supplementation (PMID 40445127).

statin_user.name: Statin User
statin_user.rationale: Statins reduce CoQ10 16-54% (PMID 30414615). 3 of 4 meta-analyses show CoQ10 100-200mg/day helps muscle symptoms. Statins do NOT deplete vitamin D (PMID 36416841 — VITAL trial negative).

ppi_user.name: PPI User (Omeprazole, Nexium, etc.)
ppi_user.rationale: PPIs deplete 5 nutrients via acid-dependent absorption mechanisms. Magnesium OR 1.71 (PMID 25023992), B12 OR 1.65 (PMID 23325883), calcium hip fracture RR 1.30.

metformin_user.name: Metformin User
metformin_user.rationale: Metformin reduces B12 by ~77 pg/mL (PMID 27130885). 5.8-30% of users become deficient. Combined with PPI: 34% greater reduction.

birth_control_user.name: Oral Contraceptive User
birth_control_user.rationale: Depletes B6 (mood via tryptophan metabolism), B12, folate (critical pre-pregnancy), magnesium, zinc. Methylfolate preferred over folic acid for 40% with MTHFR variants.

anxiety_sleep.name: Anxiety + Sleep Stack
anxiety_sleep.rationale: Three supplements targeting anxiety and sleep through different mechanisms: magnesium (NMDA antagonist + GABA agonist), ashwagandha KSM-66 (cortisol modulation via HPA axis), L-theanine (alpha-wave promotion + GABA/serotonin support). No known negative interactions between the three. Total cost ~$0.68/day.
